## reference · ux tooltip help affordance

* Tooltips are user-triggered messages providing additional information about a page element or feature, usually initiated by mouse-hover or keyboard-hover gestures.
* Tooltips are not suitable for information vital to task completion; use them for supplementary explanations or clarifications.
* Tooltips should be brief and helpful; avoid obvious or redundant text.
* Tooltips should support both mouse and keyboard hover for accessibility.
* Use tooltip arrows when multiple elements are nearby to avoid confusion.
* Use tooltips consistently throughout your site to meet user expectations.
* Provide tooltips for unlabeled icons to help users understand their purpose.
* Ensure tooltips have moderate contrast against the background for readability.
* Position tooltips to avoid blocking related content.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/tooltip-guidelines/
